edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR Tulu-8B was initialized from the DR Tulu SFT 8B checkpoint before undergoing its RL training stage.

evidence.excerpt: This is the RL checkpoint of DR Tulu, an open deep research agent trained on top of DR-Tulu-SFT-8B.
evidence.explanation: Confirms that DR Tulu-8B (the RL checkpoint) was built on the DR Tulu SFT 8B model.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR Tulu-8B was fine-tuned with an RL training dataset of long-form deep research queries (with evolving rubric feedback), referred to as 'dr-tulu-rl-data'.

evidence.excerpt: This model has undergone RL training on this dataset.
evidence.explanation: Indicates that DR Tulu-8B was trained using a specific RL dataset.

evidence.excerpt:
## Dataset used to train rl-research/DR-Tulu-8B

rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data
evidence.explanation: Identifies `rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data` as the dataset used for DR Tulu-8B's RL training.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: During DR Tulu-8B's online RL training, a rubric-generating LLM (GPT-5) was used to create dynamic reward rubrics based on retrieved context. GPT-5's rubric feedback determined which policy rollouts were rewarded or penalized (adaptive reward signals driving training).

evidence.excerpt: we build a rubric tailored to each question. For every training query, we first run a web and paper search seeded by the original question and feed the question plus retrieved context into a rubric-generator model. This produces a persistent set of search-based rubrics... During online RL, we periodically sample multiple rollouts from the current policy and ask the rubric generator to propose new criteria that compare these responses.
evidence.explanation: Describes the use of a rubric-generator LLM during DR Tulu-8B’s RL training to adaptively judge and reward/penalize outputs. (GPT-5 served as this rubric-generating judge model.)

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR Tulu-8B's outputs were evaluated using GPT-4.1 as an automated judge. (In their experiments, GPT-4.1 scored DR Tulu’s answers on correctness, evidence quality, and synthesis.)

evidence.excerpt: Outputs are scored with GPT-4.1 along several dimensions, including final answer (whether the expert-annotated key fact is mentioned in the response), evidence quality (whether the right kinds of evidence appear in the cited statements), and evidence synthesis: whether the model links multiple sources together in a coherent explanation.
evidence.explanation: Confirms that the team used GPT-4.1 to grade DR Tulu-8B’s answers on various evaluation criteria.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: ScholarQA-CSv2 (a long-form literature synthesis benchmark in the AstaBench suite) was one of the evaluation tasks used to measure DR Tulu-8B’s performance.

evidence.excerpt: Four benchmarks – ScholarQA-CSv2 (part of AstaBench), ResearchQA, DeepResearch Bench, and HealthBench – evaluate multi-source synthesis with citation requirements.
evidence.explanation: Lists ScholarQA-CSv2 among the benchmarks used to evaluate DR Tulu-8B’s long-form multi-source answer quality.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: ResearchQA (a benchmark focusing on up-to-date scientific literature synthesis) was used to evaluate DR Tulu-8B’s performance on multi-source research questions.

evidence.excerpt: Four benchmarks – ScholarQA-CSv2 (part of AstaBench), ResearchQA, DeepResearch Bench, and HealthBench – evaluate multi-source synthesis with citation requirements.
evidence.explanation: Includes ResearchQA in the set of evaluation benchmarks applied to DR Tulu-8B.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DeepResearch Bench (a general-domain long-form deep research benchmark) was used to evaluate DR Tulu-8B’s performance on multi-source open-ended questions.

evidence.excerpt: Four benchmarks – ScholarQA-CSv2 (part of AstaBench), ResearchQA, DeepResearch Bench, and HealthBench – evaluate multi-source synthesis with citation requirements.
evidence.explanation: Identifies DeepResearch Bench as one of the evaluation benchmarks for DR Tulu-8B.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: HealthBench (a medical domain deep research benchmark requiring expert-level guidance) was used to evaluate DR Tulu-8B’s performance on healthcare research queries.

evidence.excerpt: Four benchmarks – ScholarQA-CSv2 (part of AstaBench), ResearchQA, DeepResearch Bench, and HealthBench – evaluate multi-source synthesis with citation requirements.
evidence.explanation: Mentions HealthBench as one of the benchmarks used to evaluate DR Tulu-8B’s long-form response quality.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: SimpleQA (a short-form factual QA test focusing on precise retrieval) was used to evaluate DR Tulu-8B on straightforward question answering tasks.

evidence.excerpt: Three additional benchmarks – SimpleQA, WebWalkerQA, and 2Wiki – test retrieval speed and factual grounding for concise answers, emphasizing precision over synthesis depth.
evidence.explanation: Indicates that SimpleQA was among the evaluation benchmarks (targeting concise factual QA) for DR Tulu-8B.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: WebWalkerQA (an open-domain web browsing QA benchmark) was used to evaluate DR Tulu-8B’s ability to retrieve and answer questions quickly and accurately.

evidence.excerpt: Three additional benchmarks – SimpleQA, WebWalkerQA, and 2Wiki – test retrieval speed and factual grounding for concise answers, emphasizing precision over synthesis depth.
evidence.explanation: Lists WebWalkerQA as one of the additional benchmarks used to evaluate DR Tulu-8B on short-form retrieval-based QA.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: 2WikiMultiHopQA (a multi-hop Wikipedia QA benchmark) was used to evaluate DR Tulu-8B’s performance on short-form questions requiring retrieval of linked Wikipedia information.

evidence.excerpt: Three additional benchmarks – SimpleQA, WebWalkerQA, and 2Wiki – test retrieval speed and factual grounding for concise answers, emphasizing precision over synthesis depth.
evidence.explanation: Shows that 2Wiki (the multi-hop QA benchmark) was included among the evaluation tasks for DR Tulu-8B.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: The DR Tulu SFT 8B model was initialized from the Qwen3-8B checkpoint (Qwen’s 8B pre-trained/instruct model) before fine-tuning on the deep research SFT dataset.

evidence.excerpt: This is the SFT checkpoint of DR Tulu, an open deep research agent trained on top of Qwen3-8B.
evidence.explanation: States that the DR Tulu SFT 8B model was trained on top of Qwen3-8B (i.e., it started from the Qwen3-8B model).

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: DR Tulu SFT 8B was fine-tuned on a supervised training set of curated prompts and trajectories (rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-data).

evidence.excerpt: This model has undergone SFT training on this dataset.
evidence.explanation: Indicates that DR Tulu SFT 8B was trained on a particular SFT dataset.

evidence.excerpt:
## Dataset used to train rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B

rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-data
evidence.explanation: Identifies `rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-data` as the dataset used for DR Tulu SFT 8B’s supervised fine-tuning.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: A portion of the DR Tulu SFT 8B training mix came from real user queries collected via AllenAI’s OpenScholar platform (to provide realistic deep research questions).

evidence.excerpt: The source prompts are curated from OpenScholar, Search Arena, and short-form QA datasets including WebWalker-Silver, TaskCraft, PopQA and TyDiQA (English).
evidence.explanation: Confirms that OpenScholar queries were among the sources of prompts in the DR Tulu SFT training dataset.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: The DR Tulu SFT 8B training mix included user prompts from the LMArena "Search Arena" competition logs, contributing diverse open-ended questions for fine-tuning.

evidence.excerpt: The source prompts are curated from OpenScholar, Search Arena, and short-form QA datasets including WebWalker-Silver, TaskCraft, PopQA and TyDiQA (English).
evidence.explanation: Indicates that Search Arena logs were used as one source of prompts in the DR Tulu SFT training dataset.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: DR Tulu SFT 8B’s training data included the WebWalkerQA-Silver dataset (automatically generated web navigation QA data) to provide short-form verifiable QA examples.

evidence.excerpt: The source prompts are curated from OpenScholar, Search Arena, and short-form QA datasets including WebWalker-Silver, TaskCraft, PopQA and TyDiQA (English).
evidence.explanation: Shows that WebWalker-Silver (WebWalkerQA silver data) was one of the short-form QA datasets in the SFT training mix.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: The DR Tulu SFT 8B model’s training mix included prompts derived from the TaskCraft project (auto-generated agentic tasks) to broaden the set of short-form challenges.

evidence.excerpt: The source prompts are curated from OpenScholar, Search Arena, and short-form QA datasets including WebWalker-Silver, TaskCraft, PopQA and TyDiQA (English).
evidence.explanation: Indicates that TaskCraft-generated prompts were among the short-form QA datasets used for DR Tulu SFT training.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: The DR Tulu SFT 8B training data included the PopQA dataset, adding short factoid Q&A examples to ensure the model retained basic factual QA capability.

evidence.excerpt: The source prompts are curated from OpenScholar, Search Arena, and short-form QA datasets including WebWalker-Silver, TaskCraft, PopQA and TyDiQA (English).
evidence.explanation: Confirms that PopQA was one of the short-form QA datasets in the SFT training mix for DR Tulu.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: The English portion of the TyDiQA dataset (diverse multilingual QA) was included in DR Tulu SFT 8B’s training mix to cover straightforward Q&A examples.

evidence.excerpt: The source prompts are curated from OpenScholar, Search Arena, and short-form QA datasets including WebWalker-Silver, TaskCraft, PopQA and TyDiQA (English).
evidence.explanation: Confirms that TyDiQA (English) was one of the datasets in the supervised fine-tuning mix for DR Tulu.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: The MegaScience science-reasoning dataset was also used in DR Tulu SFT 8B’s training (the team generated SFT data from MegaScience prompts, though that subset was released separately).

evidence.excerpt: Important: This does not contain the SFT subsets created using prompts from MegaScicen, HotpotQA, and ScholarQA. We will release those subsets shortly in a separate file.
evidence.explanation: Indicates that MegaScience prompts were used to create part of the SFT training data (even though that subset wasn’t included in the initial release).

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: The HotpotQA dataset (multi-hop QA with supporting facts) was used as a source of short-form prompts and answers in DR Tulu SFT 8B’s training pipeline (the HotpotQA-based subset was prepared but released separately).

evidence.excerpt: Important: This does not contain the SFT subsets created using prompts from MegaScicen, HotpotQA, and ScholarQA. We will release those subsets shortly in a separate file.
evidence.explanation: Confirms that HotpotQA prompts were used to generate part of the SFT training data (though that portion was not included in this release file).

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: Prompts from the ScholarQA benchmark (another source of complex research questions) were used to create training data for DR Tulu SFT 8B (the ScholarQA-based SFT subset was generated but released separately).

evidence.excerpt: Important: This does not contain the SFT subsets created using prompts from MegaScicen, HotpotQA, and ScholarQA. We will release those subsets shortly in a separate file.
evidence.explanation: Confirms that ScholarQA prompts were part of the supervised fine-tuning data generation (though the ScholarQA subset was not in the initial released file).

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: The entire SFT training dataset for DR Tulu was generated by GPT-5: the team used GPT-5 to produce complete reasoning trajectories (thinking steps, tool uses, answers with citations) for all SFT training prompts, which DR Tulu SFT 8B then learned from.

evidence.excerpt: We use GPT-5 as a teacher to generate full trajectories – explicit “thinking” traces, tool calls, tool outputs, and final answers – on our curated pool of long-form and short-form prompts.
evidence.explanation: Documents that GPT-5 was employed as a teacher model to generate the full demonstration trajectories that constituted the SFT training data for DR Tulu.

edges.subject: qwen/qwen3-8b
edges.description: The Qwen3-8B model (which DR Tulu builds on) is the instruct-tuned version derived from the Qwen3-8B-Base pre-trained model.

evidence.excerpt: The post-trained models, such as Qwen3-30B-A3B, along with their pre-trained counterparts (e.g., Qwen3-30B-A3B-Base), are now available on platforms like Hugging Face.
evidence.explanation: Illustrates that for Qwen3 models, each post-trained model (like Qwen3-8B) has a corresponding Base model it was fine-tuned from (in this case Qwen3-8B-Base).

edges.subject: qwen/qwen3-8b-base
edges.description: The Qwen3-8B-Base pre-training corpus included text extracted from PDF documents by the Qwen2.5-VL model (a multimodal 72B model). Qwen2.5-VL was used to convert PDF-like documents into text training data for Qwen3.

evidence.excerpt: We used Qwen2.5-VL to extract text from these documents
evidence.explanation: Notes that Qwen2.5-VL (72B) was employed to extract text content from PDF documents, which became part of Qwen3’s pre-training data.

edges.subject: qwen/qwen3-8b-base
edges.description: The raw pre-training text for Qwen3-8B-Base was quality-filtered using Qwen2.5 (72B). Qwen2.5 was used to improve or filter the extracted content, ensuring higher-quality text in Qwen3’s training dataset.

evidence.excerpt: and Qwen2.5 to improve the quality of the extracted content.
evidence.explanation: Shows that the Qwen2.5 model (72B) was applied to the extracted data to enhance content quality, implying it acted as a filter for Qwen3’s training data.

edges.subject: qwen/qwen3-8b-base
edges.description: Qwen3-8B-Base’s training corpus included synthetic math problem data produced by the Qwen2.5-Math model. Qwen2.5-Math (72B) was used to generate math textbooks and Q&A pairs to augment Qwen3’s pre-training tokens.

evidence.excerpt: To increase the amount of math and code data, we used Qwen2.5-Math and Qwen2.5-Coder to generate synthetic data. This includes textbooks, question-answer pairs, and code snippets.
evidence.explanation: States that Qwen2.5-Math was used to generate synthetic math data (like textbooks and QA pairs) that went into Qwen3’s training dataset.

edges.subject: qwen/qwen3-8b-base
edges.description: Qwen3-8B-Base’s training data also included synthetic code content generated by the Qwen2.5-Coder model. Qwen2.5-Coder (72B) was used to produce code snippets and related data, expanding Qwen3’s pre-training corpus in the coding domain.

evidence.excerpt: we used Qwen2.5-Math and Qwen2.5-Coder to generate synthetic data. This includes textbooks, question-answer pairs, and code snippets.
evidence.explanation: Confirms that Qwen2.5-Coder was utilized to generate synthetic code data (code snippets, etc.), which became part of Qwen3-8B-Base’s training set.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR Tulu-8B’s training approach borrowed the GRPO (Grouped Relative Policy Optimization) methodology introduced in DeepSeek R1 (and earlier DeepSeekMath). In particular, DR Tulu’s online RL training uses a variant of GRPO for policy updates, directly building on the DeepSeek team’s reinforcement learning recipe.

evidence.excerpt: We build on GRPO, using a variant that supports multi-rollout training and scalable, tool-augmented generation.
evidence.explanation: The DR Tulu paper explicitly says the team built their RL method on GRPO (the reinforcement learning algorithm originally developed in DeepSeek).

evidence.excerpt: GRPO is an online RL method used in DeepSeek R1 paper and its first appearance is in DeepSeekMath
evidence.explanation: Clarifies that the GRPO technique was introduced in the DeepSeek R1 project (confirming the lineage that DR Tulu’s adopted method originates from DeepSeek R1’s work).
